mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in the unemployment rate above 6.5 percent signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing immediate negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 6.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand collapse, leading to negative earnings revisions and prolonged equity underperformance.
failure_condition: Joint declines in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance at the 95 percent confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding two percent annualized during high inflation periods signals supply-side resilience that supports corporate margins and equity prices.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above two percent yields negative or flat 20-day returns during periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds five percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with stable unemployment indicate a successful soft landing policy path, which sustains equity valuations by avoiding recessionary shocks.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases MoM and UNRATE change < 0.2pp
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of observed instances during tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below one percent annualized triggers recession fears, causing immediate negative market repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50 percent of instances following sub-one percent annualized GDP prints.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll employment growth above 150000 signals robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity, driving positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS above 150000 consistently precedes negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling three-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation outpaces the federal funds rate, real rates turn negative, eroding purchasing power and triggering equity sell-offs as discount rates adjust upward.
failure_condition: Negative real rate environments yield positive 20-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases across all tested periods.
